reasoning: Historically, changes to the Dow Jones Industrial Average are infrequent, typically occurring every 1 to 3 years. The index recently underwent changes in February 2024 (Amazon replacing Walgreens) and November 2024 (Nvidia replacing Intel, Sherwin-Williams replacing Dow Inc). These consecutive recent changes make another removal within the next 2-3 months extremely unlikely, as the committee prefers to maintain index continuity unless forced by significant corporate events (spinoffs, mergers, or severely diminished stock prices). Thorough searches of recent news, financial reporting (WSJ, CNBC, Bloomberg, Reuters), and official S&P Dow Jones announcements from early 2025 confirm no current rumors, announcements, or credible reports of impending removals for any of the 30 component companies before July 1, 2025. Given the short timeframe of ~2 months remaining until the resolution date and the absence of any driving catalysts (like pending mega-mergers among constituents), the probability is practically at the floor. In accordance with constraints requiring probabilities between 0.05 and 0.95, I am setting the estimate at 0.05.
